phases.name: Year 1
phases.subtitle: Explore
phases.description: Introduction to child development and family science career pathways.

milestones.label: Explore child & family science careers
milestones.courseRef: FCS 160 — Introduction to Family & Consumer Sciences
milestones.description: Survey of FCS disciplines with emphasis on child development, family services, and CFLE certification pathways. Students visit community agencies and family service organizations.

milestones.label: Study human behavior foundations
milestones.description: Psychological principles including development, learning, motivation, and behavior that underpin child development and family studies coursework.

milestones.label: Understand family & social structures
milestones.description: Social institutions, family structures, diversity, and inequality. Provides sociological frameworks for understanding family dynamics across cultures and communities.

milestones.label: Develop professional communication
milestones.description: Writing skills for professional reports, case documentation, and family service program proposals.

phases.name: Year 2
phases.subtitle: Engage
phases.description: Child development theory, family dynamics, and community engagement build professional competencies.

milestones.label: Master child development theory
milestones.description: Physical, cognitive, social, and emotional development from conception through adolescence. Students observe children at the NC A&T Child Development Laboratory and apply developmental theory to practice.

milestones.label: Study family dynamics & relationships
milestones.description: Family systems theory, communication patterns, and relationship dynamics across the lifespan. Students analyze family case studies and develop intervention strategies.

milestones.label: Explore human sexuality education
milestones.description: Comprehensive sexuality education, reproductive health, and relationship education across cultures. Essential content area for CFLE certification.

milestones.label: Develop counseling communication
milestones.description: Communication skills for family life education, parent training workshops, and community program facilitation.

phases.name: Year 3
phases.subtitle: Develop
phases.description: Advanced family studies, practicum, and CFLE content areas develop professional expertise.

milestones.label: Study family resource management
milestones.description: Financial literacy education, consumer decision-making, and family resource management across the lifespan. Students develop family budgeting workshops and consumer education curricula.

milestones.label: Master family life education methods
milestones.description: NCFR Framework for Family Life Education, program design, and facilitation methods. Students develop and deliver family life education programs aligned with CFLE certification standards.

milestones.label: Complete family services practicum
milestones.description: Supervised field experience at a family service agency, domestic violence shelter, child advocacy center, or community-based organization. Students apply CFLE competencies in real-world settings.

milestones.label: Study family diversity & policy
milestones.description: Family structure diversity, family policy analysis, and advocacy skills. Students analyze federal and state family policies including TANF, child welfare, and family leave.

phases.name: Year 4
phases.subtitle: Launch
phases.description: Capstone research, CFLE exam preparation, and professional placement complete certification readiness.

milestones.label: Complete family studies capstone
milestones.description: Integrative capstone project demonstrating CFLE competency across all 10 NCFR content areas. Students produce professional portfolios and present research to faculty and community partners.

milestones.label: Study parent-child relationships
milestones.description: Advanced study of parenting styles, attachment theory, parent education programs, and intervention strategies for at-risk families.

milestones.label: Complete professional internship
milestones.description: Supervised internship at a family service organization, school counseling center, community agency, or faith-based family ministry. Final supervised practice experience for CFLE eligibility.

milestones.label: Prepare for CFLE certification
milestones.description: Structured preparation for the NCFR Certified Family Life Educator examination. Review of all 10 CFLE content areas with practice assessments.
